name: The Blocklands
nativeLogic: Inside the Blocklands, matter resolves into discrete workable units. Anything can be gathered, broken into its components, and recombined by a crafting-grammar that ALWAYS works if you know the recipe — stone becomes tool becomes structure by rules the world enforces literally. You can build a wall, a bridge, a shelter in minutes from gathered units. Height, gravity, and daylight behave, mostly. But the rule that never bends: day is safe, and when the dark comes, things come with it — hostile, relentless, gone by dawn. Survive the nights and the Blocklands will give you anything you can build. The GM enforces this grammar as LAW while the party is inside: gathering and crafting succeed by recipe-logic, night is a survival clock, and the domain's physics override ordinary resolution.
descriptionSeed: A pocket of the world where reality went blocky and buildable — hills that resolve into stackable units, trees that break into gatherable wood, a landscape that is the most generous workshop imaginable by day and a killing field by night. Order and creation both run hot here: everything is craftable, everything is lawful, and the one law you break at your peril is the dark. Crafters come to learn the grammar; some never leave, addicted to a world that always builds what you ask.
encounterFlavor: Generative order with a clock — a structure raised in minutes, a recipe discovered, a rush to wall-in before dusk, the first sounds in the dark, a crafter who's been here too long and thinks in units now.
questSeeds: A crafter came to master the grammar and has been trapped by nightfall for weeks, walling in and mining out, slowly forgetting the world isn't made of blocks; getting them out means getting them to WANT out
The block-grammar can build something the outside world needs — a structure, a device — that can only be made here, and only between dawns; a race against the dark
The Blocklands is bleeding — block-logic diffusing into the Gearlands next door, and things are starting to resolve into units where they shouldn't; finding why the pocket is spreading
Something in the night here is not a monster but a person, changed by too many dusks; the dark isn't only hostile, and that's worse
axisVectorNote: Derived from authored poleIntensity against world_node_atlas.axisOrder (SNG-180). Disposition — what this place IS. Distinct from world position.
